sub_mechanisms: M_1: place 10 experts at vertices of a fixed 3-regular graph with girth 5
M_2: mask soft-routing weights to the 3-regular adjacency structure
M_3: chromatic-3 partition of experts; group-consistency loss across colors
M_4: vertex-transitive symmetry constraint on routing matrix
stripped_llm_application: Place 10 experts at vertices of a small fixed graph with each vertex degree 3 and no short cycles; mask soft-routing weights to only allow routing along graph edges; partition experts into 3 color-groups and add a group-consistency loss; constrain the routing matrix to respect the graph's vertex-symmetry.
novelty_claim_stripped: Topology-aware MoE (filtration-based, curvature-based, geometric) uses topological descriptors of input data; this candidate uses a FIXED structured-graph adjacency on the EXPERT side as a hard mask, with group-consistency loss tied to graph coloring — distinct from input-side topology methods.
